title: Tournament bracket report
description: Data contract for the bracket format: a knockout / single-or-double-elimination tournament laid out as rounds in columns converging left-to-right to a single champion. Each match is a card holding two competitor rows (seed, name, score) with the winner row highlighted and the loser dimmed; SVG connector elbows link each match's winner to its next-round match. The renderer reads exactly these fields, lays out the rounds, computes connector geometry at render time (never from the data), and wires match-click → detail panel, highlight-a-competitor's-path, pick-a-winner → live advance, pan/zoom, and a round filter. The champion is COMPUTED from the final match's winner — it is not a data field. The sample's #report-data is the canonical instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome metadata (brand-neutral). Use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

org.description: Owning organisation. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Independent Research) when brand-neutral.

project.description: Project / programme name. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Open Research) when brand-neutral.

reportType.description: Short label for the kind of knockout (e.g. 'Vendor Bake-off', 'Design Competition', 'Hackathon', 'Cup').

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst / scope note shown beside the title.

date.description: As-of date (ISO yyyy-mm-dd or display string).

author.description: Preparer. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Research Desk) when brand-neutral.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; sets <html data-theme>. Default for this format is plum.

format.description: Elimination format. 'single' (default) = one bracket, one loss eliminates. 'double' = an upper (winners) bracket plus a lower (consolation / losers) bracket whose matches carry bracket:'lower'; the renderer lays the lower bracket as a second band below the upper bracket. The sample is single-elim, but the schema and renderer must not break for double.

scoreUnit.description: Optional short label describing what a match score means (shown in the detail panel and tooltips), e.g. 'pts', 'goals', 'votes'. Purely cosmetic.

rounds.description: The knockout rounds, ordered from the first round (lowest order, left-most column) to the final (highest order, right-most column). For a 16-competitor single-elim bracket this is Round of 16 → Quarterfinals → Semifinals → Final. Each round becomes one column of match cards.

id.description: Stable unique round id, referenced by matches[].roundId.

name.description: Display name of the round (column header).

order.description: Left-to-right column position; 0 = first round (left-most), increasing toward the final (right-most). Determines column order regardless of array order.

bracket.description: Which bracket band this round belongs to in a double-elim layout. Omit or 'upper' for single-elim and the winners bracket; 'lower' places the round in the consolation band beneath.

matches.description: Every match in the tournament. Each match belongs to a round (roundId) and holds two competitor sides a and b. A match's winner advances along feedsInto to its next-round match; the renderer draws a connector elbow from the winner side to that next match. The single match in the final round determines the champion (no separate champion field). 'scheduled' matches (no winner yet) expose a pick-a-winner control that advances a side LIVE; reset restores the data.

id.description: Stable unique match id (DOM key; target of other matches' feedsInto).

roundId.description: The id of the round (column) this match sits in.

slot.description: Vertical position of this match within its round column, 0-based top-to-bottom. Drives vertical placement so that each pair of matches converges into one match in the next round (next-round slot = floor(slot/2)). Connector geometry is computed from layout, never from pixels in the data.

a.description: The top competitor row of the match card.

b.description: The bottom competitor row of the match card.

winner.description: Which side won ('a' or 'b'). Required when status is 'done'; omit for 'live'/'scheduled'. The winning row is highlighted with the accent; the losing row is dimmed.

status.description: Match state. 'done' = decided (winner set, scores final). 'live' = in progress (scores may be partial, no winner yet). 'scheduled' = not started (no scores/winner; gets a pick-a-winner control to advance a side live).

feedsInto.description: The id of the next-round match this match's winner advances into. Omit for the final (its winner is the champion). The renderer draws the connector elbow to this match and, when a winner is picked, seeds that next match's a or b side (by slot parity) with the advancing competitor.

time.description: Optional scheduled / played date-time shown in the detail panel.

venue.description: Optional venue / stage / track label shown in the detail panel.

note.description: Optional short note about the match (decision rationale, headline) for the detail panel.
examples: Won on TCO and security score; latency tie broken by p99.

side.description: One competitor entry on a side of a match. A side may be 'empty' (no name yet) when it is fed by an undecided earlier match — the renderer shows a placeholder until a winner is picked upstream.

seed.description: The competitor's seeding number (1 = top seed). Shown as a small mono seed chip. Omit for placeholder/empty sides.

name.description: Competitor / team / option name. Omit (or empty) for a side still awaiting an upstream winner.

score.description: This side's score in the match. Present for 'done' (final) and may be present for 'live' (partial); omit for 'scheduled'.

detail.description: Optional one-line descriptor (region, stack, group) shown in the detail panel and the highlight path.

matches.note: Top seed cruised on TCO and security.
